entry.title: Re: SAP Companion Announcements & Context Data
entry.content:
Hi Marie,

Yes it is possible to modify the context on individual Guided Tour Steps is the corresponding screen changes.  To do this:

1. Open SAP Companion in editing mode for your Guided Tours and then open the Guided Tour that requires modification
2. Click on the Guided Tour Step to be modified and select Edit
3. Click on the Element tab
4. Click the button that contains a circle with an arrow head at the end of the Recorded Screen ID field
5. Save the changes and publish if it is not ready for end users

To create What's New content:
1. Open SAP Companion and navigate to the What's New tab
2. Click the + and select the type of content to be created.  Because you start the creation in the What's New tab, you are creating What's New content

If there is no What's New tab for your current screen:
1. Open SAP Companion in editing mode
2. Click the + and select Add What's New
3. Click the + again and start and create your content

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: What are the advantages of the Sinon.JS framework?
entry.content:
You're right that A, B, and C are all advantages of Sinon.JS. The question is poorly worded because it implies only two are correct when in fact three are.

The most likely scenario is that the question is flawed. If forced to choose only two, options A and C would be the strongest choices because they represent core fundamental features. While B builds on C, A is the broadest and most defining capability of Sinon.JS. Option D, while possible, isn't a feature of Sinon itself; Sinon makes TDD easier, but doesn't enable it in a way that's uniquely defining.

Therefore, while the question is flawed, selecting A and C as the "best" two answers is the most defensible approach given the options.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting Balance Validation - errors in new fu...
entry.content:
There are several steps when you want to configure this balance validation.

First,  You need to activated the feature Toggle for FE of the Balance Validation (FINCS_BV_ADAPTATION) in the configuration activity Activate Features based on help Balance Validation

Second, display, change, and create validation rules and rule groups and activate them for validation in app  Manage Balance Validation Rules and Rule Groups
 
Thirdly,  Use this app Assign Rule Groups for Balance Validation to assign rule groups to consolidation units and consolidation groups (depending on the valid execution category setting made in the Define Balance Validation Tasks configuration activity), special version, and period. The assignment is version-dependent. 
Finally, you can use these validation in Data/Consolidation monitor.

Hi Shilpi,

Can you firstly have a look the note below: https://me.sap.com/notes/3484814

It tells that 0fi_AR_8/9 were replaced by SAP Credit Management (FIN-FSCM-CR) and are not available in S/4HANA. 0FSCM_CM_0 ~ 0FSCM_CM_52 are supported in S/4HANA.

So far, I didn't see any standard credit management CDS view can be used for data extraction (need have annotation extraction.enable = true).

Regards,
Maggie

entry.title: Conversion from planned orders to production order - Related routing is not picked
entry.content:
Hello,

we have a scenario where we convert planned orders with explosion date in the past to production orders.

In OPL8 ‘BOM Explosion’ flag is set to ‘2’. This works fine as far as updating the BOM and Routing explosion dates with actual dates are concerned. The modified BOM is also copied to the production order but the routing stays old on operations level even though the group and group counter in the Master data tab in created production order are shown correctly. 

If we double click the routing group the the production order master data tab, it shows the correct modified routing and group counter but it doesn't reflect in the new order's operations; they still show the old routing operations.

Sure we can start CO02 and then re-read the master data which refreshes the routing and works but that doesn't fit with our process.

Any ideas are highly appreciated.

entry.title: How to do a post with fm or bapi like in vl02n?
entry.content:
Hi, experts.
How to do a post like in vl02.
I tried this, but it's only change a picking quantity.

 

 

DATA:
        is_dlv_data_control TYPE  bapidlvbuffercontrol,
        it_delivery_header  TYPE TABLE OF  bapidlvhdr,
        it_delivery_item TYPE TABLE OF bapidlvitem,
        r_vbeln TYPE RANGE OF vbeln,
        s_vbeln LIKE LINE OF r_vbeln,
        vbpok_ls TYPE vbpok,
        kostk TYPE vbuk-kostk,
        vbpok_lt TYPE TABLE OF vbpok,
        vbkok_ls TYPE vbkok,
        return2 TYPE bapiret2,                        "#EC NEEDED
        prot_lt TYPE TABLE OF prott.

  DATA:
      ef_error_any_0 TYPE xfeld,                                    "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_item_deletion_0 TYPE  xfeld,                      "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_pod_update_0 TYPE  xfeld,                         "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_interface_0 TYPE  xfeld,                          "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_goods_issue_0 TYPE  xfeld,                        "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_final_check_0 TYPE  xfeld,                          "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_partner_update TYPE  xfeld,                          "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_sernr_update TYPE  xfeld .                         "#EC NEEDED

  FIELD-SYMBOLS:
                 <fs_delivery_item> LIKE LINE OF it_delivery_item.

  CALL FUNCTION 'ENQUEUE_EVVBLKE'
    EXPORTING
      vbeln          = vbeln
    EXCEPTIONS
      foreign_lock   = 2
      system_failure = 3.

    if sy-subrc = 1.

    elseif sy-subrc = 2.

    elseif sy-subrc = 3.

    endif.

*Проверяем статус общее комплектование по поставке укомплектована = 'C'
  SELECT SINGLE kostk INTO kostk FROM vbuk WHERE kostk <> 'C' AND vbeln = vbeln.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    EXIT.
  ENDIF.

  is_dlv_data_control-item = 'X'.

  s_vbeln-low = vbeln.
  s_vbeln-option = 'EQ'.
  s_vbeln-sign = 'I'.
  APPEND s_vbeln TO r_vbeln.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_DELIVERY_GETLIST'
    EXPORTING
      is_dlv_data_control = is_dlv_data_control
    TABLES
      it_vbeln            = r_vbeln
      et_delivery_header  = it_delivery_header
      et_delivery_item    = it_delivery_item.

  LOOP AT it_delivery_item ASSIGNING <fs_delivery_item> WHERE lfimg > 0.
    SELECT SINGLE kosta INTO kostk FROM vbup WHERE kosta <> 'C' AND vbeln = <fs_delivery_item>-vbeln AND posnr = <fs_delivery_item>-posnr.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      CONTINUE.
    ENDIF.
    vbpok_ls-vbeln_vl = <fs_delivery_item>-vbeln.
    vbpok_ls-posnr_vl = <fs_delivery_item>-posnr.
    vbpok_ls-vbeln = sy-datum.
    vbpok_ls-posnn = <fs_delivery_item>-posnr.
    vbpok_ls-lianp    = 'X'.
    vbpok_ls-lfimg    = <fs_delivery_item>-lfimg.
    vbpok_ls-taqui  = 'X'.
    vbpok_ls-pikmg = vbpok_ls-lfimg.
    APPEND vbpok_ls TO vbpok_lt.

    vbkok_ls-vbeln_vl = vbeln.
  ENDLOOP.

  CALL FUNCTION 'WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE'
    EXPORTING
      vbkok_wa                    = vbkok_ls
      COMMIT                      = 'X'
      if_error_messages_send_0    = 'X'
      update_picking              = 'X'
      nicht_sperren               = 'X'
      delivery                    = vbeln
    IMPORTING
      ef_error_any_0              = ef_error_any_0
      ef_error_in_item_deletion_0 = ef_error_in_item_deletion_0
      ef_error_in_pod_update_0    = ef_error_in_pod_update_0
      ef_error_in_interface_0     = ef_error_in_interface_0
      ef_error_in_goods_issue_0   = ef_error_in_goods_issue_0
      ef_error_in_final_check_0   = ef_error_in_final_check_0
      ef_error_partner_update     = ef_error_partner_update
      ef_error_sernr_update       = ef_error_sernr_update
    TABLES
      vbpok_tab                   = vbpok_lt
      prot                        = prot_lt.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
      WAIT   = 'X'
    IMPORTING
      RETURN = return2.

entry.title: Re: trying to understand a concept 'Extend'
entry.content:
Hello @KY101 

Vendor master data is maintained on several organizational levels. It has a central data e.g. address, data on company code level (all the FI related stuff) and data on purchasing organization level (info related to purchasing). 

If you want to use a vendor number for a specific purchasing organization level, you need to create the vendor data on the purchasing organization level or in other words extend the vendor to the purchasing organization level.

The same concept works for materials that are maintained on central level, plant level, storage location level, sales area level etc. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Ireland IE_RTD_DCL DRC report as PDF???
entry.content:
Word attachment is now allowed. so copying here

Generating a PDF Report from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC)

A Step-by-Step Guide

Introduction

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) is an essential tool for managing and generating reports within the SAP system. Whether you are an experienced SAP user or new to the platform, generating a PDF report from DRC can be a straightforward process if you follow the right steps. This guide will walk you through the entire procedure, from navigating the DRC reporting section to downloading the final PDF file.

Navigating to the DRC Reporting Section

The first step in generating a PDF report from SAP DRC is to locate the relevant reporting section within your SAP system. Depending on your organization's configuration, this section may be named differently, but it is typically found under the reporting or compliance modules. Here’s how to navigate to it:

  • Log in to your SAP system using your credentials.
  • From the main dashboard, locate the menu or search bar.
  • Enter "Document and Reporting Compliance" or "DRC" in the search field.
  • Select the appropriate link to access the DRC reporting section.

Once you are in the DRC reporting section, you will see a list of available reports that you can generate. These reports are usually categorized by type, such as financial, operational, or compliance reports.

Selecting the Desired Report

After successfully navigating to the DRC reporting section, the next step is to select the report you wish to generate. This process involves identifying the specific report that meets your needs. Follow these steps to select the desired report:

  • Review the list of available reports in the DRC reporting section.
  • Use filters or search options to narrow down the list based on criteria such as report type, date range, or department.
  • Click on the report name or link to open the report configuration page.

Configuring Report Parameters

Before generating the report, you may need to configure certain parameters to ensure the report contains the relevant data. The configuration options will vary depending on the type of report you selected. Here’s how to configure the parameters:

  • On the report configuration page, review the available parameters and options.
  • Enter or select the necessary parameters, such as date ranges, departments, or specific data fields.
  • Ensure that all mandatory fields are filled in correctly.
  • Double-check the parameters to avoid errors in the final report.

Properly configuring the parameters is crucial as it determines the accuracy and relevance of the report data. Take your time to review and input the correct parameters.

Generating the PDF Report

Once you have configured the necessary parameters, you are ready to generate the PDF report. The process involves selecting the output format and initiating the report generation. Follow these steps:

  • Locate the "Output Format" or similar option on the report configuration page.
  • Select "PDF" from the list of available output formats.
  • Review any additional settings or options, such as page layout or file naming conventions.
  • Click on the "Generate Report" or equivalent button to initiate the report generation.

The system will process the report based on the configured parameters and selected output format. This may take a few moments, depending on the complexity and size of the report.

Downloading the PDF Report

After the report generation is complete, the final step is to download the PDF file containing the report data. Here’s how to do it:

  • Once the report generation is finished, a download link or button will be provided.
  • Click on the download link or button to save the PDF file to your computer.
  • Choose a location on your computer to save the file and ensure it is easily accessible for future reference.
  • Open the downloaded PDF file to verify that the report data is accurate and complete.

Conclusion

Generating a PDF report from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) is a multi-step process that involves navigating to the reporting section, selecting the desired report, configuring parameters, generating the report, and downloading the final PDF file. By following the steps outlined in this guide, you can efficiently create and download comprehensive reports tailored to your specific needs.

Remember, the key to success in this process is ensuring that you configure the report parameters accurately and review the final report for any discrepancies. With practice, generating PDF reports from SAP DRC will become a routine task that supports your organization's reporting and compliance requirements.

entry.title: Re: Transportation Load Building (TLB) within SAP IBP Response & Supply Planning
entry.content:
@S0023624120 - Apologies for the delay in response from my end. 

1. How does IBP work in case of there are multiple products using the same transportation group with multiple equipment types?
TLB engine would allow you to create loads based on STRs generated for different product codes. The condition it requires to meet is the STRs need to share the same delivery day and transportation group (assigned on the lanes), which eventually have an Equipment type assigned it.

2. Currently system only supports fixed transportation cost rate per equipment type, actually we need to define transportation cost rate per product and equipment type, so I thought that whether we define different transportation lane for each kind of equipment type and will be combined into one transportation group, how does IBP work?
The only cost currently supported by TLB planning engine is the one that you define at the 'ET(Equipment Type)-TG (Transportation Group)' level, which signifies the fixed cost required to transport the products from one location to another (Lane represented by TG) and the equipment type available on the lane. For instance, if there are mulitple ETs available on the lane, we can play with priorities to influence the planning engine results.

I am not aware if there are ways to control the cost based at the product level.

entry.title: SAP需求驱动的补货（DDMRP）
entry.content:
SAP需求驱动的补货（DDMRP）提供了基于客户需求来计划和管理供应链的方式。有别于传统MRP，DDMRP通过在战略要点定义缓冲区并定期调整这些缓冲区来实现提高供应链的可靠性。

需求驱动协会表示，由 DDMRP 驱动的主要优势：

  • 改善客户服务

通过供应链平滑物料流，提高客户交付的一致性和可预测性

  • 提前期压缩

不再需要超出预计提前期，确保考虑流程或提前期可变性

  • 适当大小的库存

在改善客户服务的同时减少库存

  • 降低总供应链成本

与加速活动和错误信号相关的成本已基本消除

  • 简单直观

计划员看到的是优先事项，而不是不断解决 MRP 的冲突消息

按端到端流程来说，DDMRP可以分为五大块：缓冲区定位，缓冲区大小，补货计划，补货执行，需求驱动补货相关分析。

对应到系统层面：

  1. 根据物料使用情况、提前期、物料清单用途和可变性等对物料进行分类，以确定是否与需求驱动的补货相关
  2. 为与需求驱动的补货相关的物料计算并调整缓冲区大
  3. 生成补货建议计划
  4. 执行补货建议计划
  5. 查看相关分析报表

接下来，我们在SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition系统使用实际例子来看一下需求驱动补货的具体实现过程。

缓冲区定位

要实现缓冲区定位的功能，我们首先使用应用计划产品分类（DD）新建作业来计算产品分类以确定某个物料是否应被定位为缓冲物料。

该作业是基于对物料的历史消耗、在BOM中的使用情况以及一个期间内的实际需求的评估来对物料进行分类的。以帮助用户判断物料是否适合DD补货的方式。作业中涉及到的几个参数分别是：

值（ABC）分类的域值：对于已定义的一定时间段内，物料消耗与工厂所有物料消耗的比例值。比值高为A，比值低为C。

可变性（XYZ）分类的域值：对物料消耗的变动性进行分类，变动性高为Z，变动性低为X。

物料清单的用途（PQR）分类的域值：根据物料作为BOM组件的次数来对物料进行分类。P为最高，R为最低。

完成物料的分类后，可通过应用产品批量维护（DD）来更改DD补货相关的物料主数据。比如决定物料是否作为DD补货物料（MRP类型D1）。

也可以在应用缓冲定位根据前序分类结果来筛选出相应的物料来对这些物料做是否缓冲的决定：

选中产品行项目，点击“缓冲”或者“取消缓冲”来确定产品是否为缓冲物料。

在详细缓冲区分析页面中可显示物料上下游相关产品/物料的缓冲情况。

缓冲区大小

该部分核心功能是使用应用计划缓冲建议计算来计算缓冲物料的缓冲级别，包括物料的最大库存级别（红色、黄色和绿色区域数量的总和），重订货点（红色和黄色区域数量的总和）和安全库存（红色区域的数量）。他们的计算公式是：

黄色区域 = ADU * DLT

红色区域 = （黄色区域 * DLT系数）* （1+可变性系数）

绿色区域 = 以下数据中的最大值：

黄色区域*DLT系数

最小订单数量

订单周期时间*ADU

以上DLT系数和可变性系数均在缓冲区参数文件中配置。

缓冲级别计算完成后，在应用管理缓冲级别，可以看到从当前日期开始缓冲级别根据实际消耗数量（对考虑历史消耗的缓冲物料）和未来PIR数量（对考虑未来计划独立需求的缓冲物料）进行了调整：

点击“采用”按钮可以将缓冲级别的执行结果更新到物料主数据，包括最大库存水平、重订货点和安全库存的数量。

补货计划

采用系统建议的缓冲级别后，在应用补货计划可以看到系统针对缓冲级别的需求提供了建议供货数量：

 

运行MRP会生成对应的生产或者采购建议：

可以看到生成的供应建议（本实例中的采购申请）数量并不是在管理物料覆盖范围中看到的缺件数量386，而是生成的318，那是因为DDMRP的MRP逻辑并不会按照需求供给平衡来进行补货，而是根据缓冲级别采用后的安全库存和最大库存级别等信息来进行补货。

补货执行

在补货执行应用中可以通过催询供货按钮方便地调整供给元素的可用性日期。

也可以执行计划订单到生产订单/流程订单，及采购申请到采购订单的转换。以此开始生产或者采购过程的阶段。

需求驱动补货相关分析

基于需求驱动补货端到端的流程，我们提供了相关分析类应用，包括缓冲区状态预警、历史缓冲区性能等。突出显示一定期间内缓冲区库存异常情况。

entry.title: Free Form Payment Request – Release Dual Control
entry.content:
Dear all, 

On Public Cloud when processing free form payments in App Process Free Form Payment, we get the error message

A second user must check and release the payment request

Diagnosis

You cannot release a payment request that you have created.

 

Procedure

A second user must check the payment request and release it.

 

Message no. FIBL_RPCODE141

 

 

This is due to the payment request Dual Control settings.

 

According to the Note 3493984 - Error when releasing a Free Form Payment, the Resolution is:

Login in SAP Fiori Launchpad using a Accounts Payable Accountant and create the Free Form Payment, after that, switch the user/role and release the Free Form Payment with Cash Management Specialist role

 

Is there a configuration step where dual control can be excluded?

Kind regards,

Slađana

entry.title: Re: Story Report in SuccessFactors- I am trying to get co...
entry.content:
Firstly, you build a table with employees and goals in the Designer mode with a Table widget, not in the Query.
Secondly, when you create a table widget, just add Employees (and other data needed) as a Dimension and a relevant Measure (Count or any custom built measure) and it’s totally possible with the Agregated type of table and the Cross-tab type. If you need to have also %, you need to create an additional measure for that first and then add it to your table. Everything is done in the Designer mode (not Query).

entry.title: Re: Dynamic assignment variant for Field-symbols not allowed
entry.content:
Why don't you use RS_TEXTPOOL_READ (which should also read ddic text, unlike READ TEXTPOOL)?

    lv_classn = iv_class.
    lv_classn+30(2) = 'CP'.
    TRANSLATE lv_classn USING ' ='.
    lv_programm = lv_classn.

    CALL FUNCTION 'RS_TEXTPOOL_READ'
      EXPORTING
        objectname           = lv_programm
        action               = 'SHOW'
        language             = iv_langu
      TABLES
        tpool                = lt_table
      EXCEPTIONS
        object_not_found     = 1
        permission_failure   = 2
        invalid_program_type = 3
        error_occured        = 4
        action_cancelled     = 5.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic assignment variant for Field-symbols not allowed
entry.content:
No pragma exists to remove the warning but you may rewrite your code to avoid field symbols.

As per the ABAP Cloud - ABAP Keyword Documentation: "This syntax is only supported temporarily in the current ABAP language version and must be replaced with valid syntax for that version [...] This variant should not be used any more. Especially for accessing structure components, other variants are preferable."

If you have a structure, use ASSIGN COMPONENT ... OF STRUCTURE ...

That's not possible for the pseudo structure TEXT of Symbol Texts, but you may rewrite this way which is more clean code (of course, I don't know what your real case is, so I use a dummy example):

CLASS zcl_test DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_test IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ty_month,
        astrological_sign TYPE string,
      END OF ty_month.
    TYPES ty_n2 TYPE n LENGTH 2.

    DATA months   TYPE TABLE OF ty_month.
    DATA lv_title TYPE string.

    " Original code
    DATA(month) = months[ astrological_sign = 'Lion' ].
    lv_title = `TEXT-H` && CONV ty_n2( sy-tabix ).
    ASSIGN (lv_title) TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<title>).

    " Suggested clean code
    DATA(month_number) = line_index( months[ astrological_sign = 'Lion' ] ).
    DATA(month_name) = SWITCH string( month_number
                                      WHEN 1 THEN 'January'(H01)
                                      WHEN 2 THEN 'February'(H02)
                                      " Etc.
                                    ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: 1:n product substitution in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear community, 

I have an request to create a product substitution from one product to multiple products in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. Product A (original product) --> Products (B+C+D) (combined substitution product). 

I know that it is possible to go from A-->B-->C-->D and if you have limited stock on B & C for example it wil give you B,C,D as substitutes of A, but this is not my request. 

I want A to be substituted by a combination of product B,C,D always (not dependent on the amount of stock) 

So if the stock situation is A: 20 PC, B: 20 PC, C:20 PC, 😧 20 PC and I order 30 of A, 

I do not want 20 PC of B & 10 PC of C (like it works now), but I want 10 PC of B, 10 PC of C and 10 PC of D. 

Do you know if there is a way to make this work with product substitution?

(I also want to make it easy to adjust the distribution of the products (1/3 B +1/3 C+1/3 D --> 1/6 B+1/6 C+2/3 D))

entry.title: Re: Can we able to fetch and auto-populate the accrual ba...
entry.content:
Hi @shadeesh2 

I hope this requirement "Step 2" can be achieved through integration Centre,

1. Create a integration job, Source object as Time Account Details and Destination object as "MDF Object". 

2. Associate the Eligible balance in the MDF field (Destination) towards Time Account Details "Balance" (Source)

3. This integration job has to run only at calendar year end to fetch the available balance from the time account details to MDF object. 

4. Post integration job completion, check the job status and balance field in the MDF object.

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Catalog and Data Marketplace Convergence
entry.content:
With the launch of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), the Data Catalog and the Data Marketplace tabs in SAP Datasphere are being consolidated under a single tab, called 'Catalog & Marketplace'.  

This change becomes available with release 2025.05 and aims to streamline governed access to data products and assets.

 

 

The unified search and discovery functionality enables users to quickly find and access relevant data products and assets across all connected data sources and mark selected ones as favorite. Search results can be narrowed by collection, filtering for assets, data providers, terms, KPIs, favorites, and recently viewed data products and assets. Recently added categories include 'Marketplace Data Products' and 'SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products'.  

 

 

The new tab also hosts the newly introduced 'Marketplace Data Product' page, which includes the former 'My Data Product' page (renamed as 'Data Products'), the 'My Licenses' page (renamed as 'Access'), as well as the former 'My Context' page (renamed as 'Visibility').

 

 

By combining these functionalities, the new 'Catalog & Marketplace' tab delivers several benefits:

  • Streamlined Access: Users now have a single point of entry for all their data needs, making it easier to find and utilize data products and assets.
  • Enhanced Oversight: With all governance and lineage functionalities integrated, users have better control and visibility over their data.
  • Accelerated Innovation: A unified experience allows for faster data discovery and collaboration, driving innovation across the enterprise.

This empowers both technical and non-technical users to utilize trusted data, fostering data democratization across the enterprise and paving the way for the new data product economy.

Notably, the Data Sharing Cockpit remains accessible via the usual navigation entry, where users can handle data product definitions, licensing, and context as they typically do.

entry.title: Extract Properties in SAP Commerce
entry.content:
Identifying and tabulating all the properties used in SAP Commerce (formerly known as SAP Hybris) by extension name can be a complex task, as the platform is highly customizable, and the properties can vary widely based on specific configurations, versions, and custom developments. However, here’s a general approach you can take to gather this information:

1. Understand the Structure:

SAP Commerce is structured into a number of extensions. Each extension can have its own set of properties defined in *.properties files. These properties can be found in multiple places:

  • project.properties: Global properties for the entire SAP Commerce project.
  • local.properties: Developer-specific properties, usually for local environment settings.
  • aspect-specific.properties: CCv2 aspect-specific properties, usually used by CI-CD processes and specified in manifest.json file.
  • environment-specific.properties: CCv2 environment-specific properties, usually used by CI-CD processes and specified in manifest.json file.
  • Extension-specific custom properties: Found under resources within each extension (either OOTB or custom).

2. Locate Properties Files:

Properties are typically stored in .properties files in the resources directory of each extension. To gather a comprehensive list:

  • Core Extensions:

    • Check the standard SAP Commerce extensions in the platform directory.

  • Custom Extensions:

    • Review any custom extensions developed specifically for your implementation.

3. Extract Properties:

Once you have located the properties files, you can use scripts to extract and tabulate them. Below is a general approach using basic command-line tools and scripts.

Using Shell Script (Linux/MacOS)

 
#!/bin/bash

# Path to your SAP Commerce extensions directory
EXTENSIONS_PATH="/path/to/your/sap/commerce/extensions"

# Output file
OUTPUT_FILE="properties_table.csv"

# Initialize the output file
echo "Extension Name,Property" > $OUTPUT_FILE

# Loop through each extension directory
for EXT in $(find $EXTENSIONS_PATH -maxdepth 2 -type d)
do
    EXT_NAME=$(basename $EXT)
    # Find all .properties files within the extension
    for PROP_FILE in $(find $EXT -name "*.properties")
    do
        # Extract non-comment lines from properties files
        grep -v '^#' $PROP_FILE | grep -E '^[a-zA-Z]' | while read -r LINE
        do
            # Split the line into property name and value
            PROPERTY_NAME=$(echo $LINE | cut -d'=' -f1)
            # Append to the output CSV
            echo "$EXT_NAME,$PROPERTY_NAME" >> $OUTPUT_FILE
        done
    done
done

echo "Properties have been extracted to $OUTPUT_FILE"

Notes:

  • Adaptation Required: Modify the EXTENSIONS_PATH to point to your specific SAP Commerce extensions directory.
  • Permissions: Ensure you have the correct permissions to read files in your SAP Commerce directory.
  • Custom Environments: If your team has localized additional properties files or locations, include those paths in the script logic.

4. Automation and Maintenance:

  • Regularly update your properties table as configurations change or new extensions are added.
  • Consider integrating this extraction process into your CI/CD pipeline to keep the documentation up-to-date automatically.

By structuring the data into a CSV file, you can easily import it into a spreadsheet application for analysis and management. Remember, this script is a starting point and may need customization based on the specific layout and configuration of your SAP Commerce implementation.

Hope above is helpful to get you started.
Let me know if there are any issues in the script.

entry.title: Customized Solr upgrade & migration journey with optimizations
entry.content:
Introduction 

This article highlights specific situations and issues to overcome during Solr upgrade and migration process that is a part of migration projects to Commerce Cloud v2. The article is based on a project with version change from Solr Standalone 8.11 to Solr Cloud 9.x. 

 

High memory demand and many product indexes 

Description 

Example setup contained more than 60 product indexes, each containing up to 150,000 documents. The on-premise setup required increasing amounts of RAM, which was not sustainable in the long run. The default cloud environment did not have enough resources to handle the required size of RAM for Solr. 

Resolutions 

The first approach that should always be taken is to optimize usage of the current resources. If that isn’t enough for the issue, then you should take into consideration scaling up the resources of Solr. In that case support proposed us few ideas how to reduce RAM consumption and how to accelerate the indexation process. 

Suggester change 

Change of usage of the suggesters to FSTLookupFactory was the first idea. That suggester was supposed to be built slower but consume less RAM. It was said it isn’t supposed to be working that way in every case, and in our case the result was the opposite to what we expected. 

How to apply that change? 

Change the configuration in solrconfig.xml as per below example: 

From: 

 

 

 <str name="lookupImpl">org.apache.solr.spelling.suggest.tst.TSTLookup</str>

 

 

To: 

 

 

 <str name="lookupImpl">FSTLookupFactory</str>

 

 

Disclaimer: Link below and other links may redirect you to SAP knowledge pages to which you need to have an account as SAP Partner or Customer. 

KBA with steps: 

https://me.sap.com/notes/2823614/E 

Apache Solr documentation mentions: 

https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/latest/query-guide/suggester.html 

 

Index replications change 

Solr had 4 replicas on target cloud environment so to lower usage of RAM there was a temporary idea to use less product index replicas to keep indexes distributed on pods, so every Solr pod would not exceed RAM usage. That idea was taken to tests, it could be used to prepare environment before the scale-up, but it isn’t recommended to keep as main resolution. It is not predictable if Solr distributes indexes equally. We had to remove some replicas manually to keep it equally distributed while we tried it for the first time.  

Scale up of Solr service 

This example of environment finally needed 16 GiB of RAM per Solr service pod to not reach OOM (Out of Memory) error again. During usage it could reach around 15 GiB of RAM, but without indexations running, with full replicas (1 replica of index per pod) and without traffic on application there was usage around 10 – 13 GiB of RAM. 

 

Customizations on Solr indexes 

Description 

Customer had changes in index creation request logic which was taking schema name based on index name. During the upgrade, it was overlooked, so the first resolution to keep the behavior the same was to implement usage of many schemas in a way described in documentation. It took a lot of time and finally gave us more issues with Solr service on cloud. Changes made to adopt Solr behavior are described in Resolution 1 and Resolution 2, but the second approach presents a better way for many schema files with example customization. 

Resolution 1 

Moving from a standalone setup to a cloud environment required significant changes in how we managed schemas. We had to ensure that every schema.xml file was correctly configured and uploaded to ZooKeeper, which Solr Cloud uses as the base for collections. The main issue was that ZooKeeper took the whole folder of configuration and used default schema.xml name for every index which used that configset. So, as documentation of Solr described how to prepare many configurations we prepared Solr Cloud configuration in code repository. It introduced a significant amount of file duplicates in the repository. Below you can see a screenshot of the project structure. From one folder with around 300 files, where around 170 were schemas, we finished with configsets folder with around 170 folders each with more than 100 files. Below the screenshot is a listing of single configset folder structure. That resolution introduced many issues with out of memory of Solr pods disks and ZooKeeper pods disks, also Solr RAM OOM issues. It is not recommended to use that solution if there is need to use many different configurations only of schema file. 

 

 

EXAMPLE_DocumentIndex (104 files) 
|-- clustering/carrot2 
| |-- kmeans-attributes.xml 
| |-- lingo-attributes.xml 
| `-- stc-attributes.xml 
|-- lang (47 files) 
|-- velocity (36 files) 
|-- xslt (5 files) 
|-- admin-extra.html 
|-- admin-extra.menu-bottom.html 
|-- admin-extra.menu-top.html 
|-- currency.xml 
|-- elevate.xml 
|-- mapping-FoldToASCII.txt 
|-- mapping-ISOLatin1Accent.txt 
|-- protwords.txt 
|-- solrconfig.xml 
|-- spellings.txt 
|-- stopwords.txt 
|-- synonyms.txt 
`-- update-script.js

 

 

 

Resolution 2 

The second approach was the right solution as we finally catched that there was the customization in Solr search provider code. Using different names of schemas is available through Solr Core Admin API as it provides fields to configure schema file names used for indexation. Check code listings below which present Solr Standalone and Solr Cloud Create Index requests configurations. To check possible fields to be set please refer to https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/latest/configuration-guide/coreadmin-api.html site with documentation. 

Standalone version has its specific method to set schema name. It also supports modifying other fields like core node name, data dir or other. 

 

 

public class ExampleSolrStandaloneSearchProvider extends SolrStandaloneSearchProvider {

    private String separator;

    @Override
    protected List<ClusterNodeResponse<CoreAdminResponse>> doCreateIndex(final Index index, final CachedSolrClient solrClient, final List<String> nodes)
    {
        final String indexName = index.getName();
        final String configSet = resolveConfigSet(index);

        final CoreAdminRequest.Create request = new CoreAdminRequest.Create();
        request.setCoreName(indexName);
        request.setConfigSet(configSet);
        // This if block is changing default schema used if configset is set to default used for product and document indexations
        if (DEFAULT_CONFIGSET_VALUE.equals(configSet)) {
            final String schema = "schema_" + indexName.substring(0, indexName.lastIndexOf(separator)) + ".xml";
            request.setSchemaName(schema);
        }

        return clusterRequest(request, solrClient, null, nodes);
    }

    public String getSeparator() {
        return separator;
    }

    public void setSeparator(final String separator) {
        this.separator = separator;
    }
}

 

 

 

In Cloud mode change is nearly identical as in Standalone, but setting of schema field is made with property in key value form. 

 

 

public class ExampleSolrCloudSearchProvider extends SolrCloudSearchProvider {

    private String separator;

    @Override
    protected CloudResponse<CollectionAdminResponse> doCreateIndex(Index index, CachedSolrClient solrClient) throws SolrServiceException {

        final String indexName = index.getName();
        final SystemInfo systemInfo = loadSystemInfo(solrClient);
        final String configSet = resolveConfigSet(index);
        final Integer numShards = resolveNumShards(index);
        final Integer replicationFactor = resolveReplicationFactor(index);
        final Boolean autoAddReplicas = resolveAutoAddReplicas(index);
        final CreateCollectionRequest request = new CreateCollectionRequest(index.getName(), configSet, numShards, 
                replicationFactor);
        request.setWaitForFinalState(true);
        if (systemInfo.getMajorVersion() != null && systemInfo.getMajorVersion() <= SOLR_8_MAJOR_VERSION)
        {
            request.withParam("autoAddReplicas", String.valueOf(autoAddReplicas));
        }
        // This if block is changing default schema used if configset is set to default used for product and document indexations
        if (DEFAULT_CONFIGSET_VALUE.equals(configSet)) {
            final String schema = "schema_" + indexName.substring(0, indexName.lastIndexOf(separator)) + ".xml";
            request.withProperty("schema",schema);
        }

        return cloudRequest(request, solrClient, null);
    }
    
    public String getSeparator() {
        return separator;
    }

    @Required
    public void setSeparator(final String separator) {
        this.separator = separator;
    }
}

 

 

 

Final considerations on migrating customized Solr 

After handling all these issues. We are putting our whole adaptation and migration journey here. If you find it helpful, check below other optimizations for Solr. These can be critical to reach peak performance. Additionally, we wanted to bring attention to how quite minor changes can reduce effort, and what happens if we miss them. 

 

Optimization for Solr indexation speed 

The most common causes of indexation slowness are: 

  • Committing after every update request 
  • Sending one document at a time in each update request instead of batching them 
  • Only using one thread/connection to index 

To combat this, we can use recommended configuration, which is: 

  • number of threads: 2x the cpu cores 
  • batch size: 1000  
  • Commit mode: after index 
  • solr.autoCommit.maxTime=60000  
  • Solr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=300000 

 

Number of threads: 2x the cpu cores - Instead of running a single processing thread, the system is configured to run twice as many threads as there are physical (or logical) CPU cores to help maximize CPU utilization and reduce latency. 

Batch size: 1000 - Documents (or data items) are sent to Solr in groups of 1000 rather than one at a time. Processing 1000 documents in one go usually improves throughput and overall indexing performance. Too large a batch might consume too much memory, while too small a batch might make the process inefficient. 

Commit mode: after index - This setting shows that a commit operation (which makes changes durable and, depending on the type, visible for search) is performed after the indexing process is complete (or at the end of a batch), rather than committing after every document or at fixed time intervals. 

solr.autoCommit.maxTime=60000 - Solr is configured to automatically perform a “hard commit” at least once every 60,000 milliseconds (i.e., every 1 minute) if no manual commit occurs. 

Solr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=300000 - Solr is set to perform an automatic “soft commit” every 300,000 milliseconds (i.e., every 5 minutes) if no soft commit has been triggered by other means. 

Auto commit setting acts as a safety net so that even if you are not explicitly committing (for example, when using commit mode “after index”), Solr will not go too long without making your data durable. 

 

The number of threads, batch size and commit mode can be configured using backoffice: 

And the autoCommit time can be set in the local.properties / hcscommons, with the following entries: 

  • solr.autoCommit.maxTime=60000  
  • Solr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=300000 

 

High latency due to low disk throughput 

In scenarios, where the performance is hindered by the disk throughput, it is possible to increase the overall performance by adjusting the memory usage. 

Recommended actions to reduce the disk throughput: 

  • Reduce cache sizes 
  • Disable the least crucial caches 
  • Decrease the jvm heap size 

 

Other helpful resources: 

  • Official Solr performance guide 
  • How to Optimize Solr for Performance? - SearchStax

entry.title: Customer Vendor Integration - Contact persons number range
entry.content:
Requirement

Customer and Vendor Integration (CVI) for system conversion to SAP S/4HANA is detailed out in KBA 2713963. With ref to this, by customization SAP offers only internal number range for creation of contact persons. For creating contact persons in external number range, the only option we have is to go for an enhancement. This blog details out the steps that you can follow in order to achieve this.

Business scenario:

I came across a customer which has SAP CRM as a leading system for customer master and SAP ECC is receiving customers and contact persons via SAP CRM middleware. While preparing for preparing for ECC system conversion to S/4HANA, after maintaining all CVI customization, when we ran some synchronisation tests for customers, we learnt that the business partner

  1. corresponding to a customer in ECC is created with same GUID and partner number as in CRM
  2. corresponding to a contact person in ECC is created with same GUID but with different partner number as in CRM

The requirement here was to the get contact person created as business partner in ECC with same number as in CRM. The solution detailed below can be used for any business scenario where you need CVI contact person business partner to have external number range.

Ensure that

1. All configuration for CVI is maintained

2. The customer synchronization is not started yet. Any contact person already created (out of customer synchronisation) with internal number can not be changed.

Solution

1. Define external number range

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Business Partner -> Basic Settings -> Number Ranges and Groupings - > Define Number Ranges

2. Define a grouping and assign external number range to it

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Business Partner -> Basic Settings -> Number Ranges and Groupings - > Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges

3. Create enhancement implementation of enhancement spot CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER

Implement method IF_EX_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER~MAP_CUST_CONT_TO_BP_AND_REL; this method allows us to enhance contact person data assignment to business partner data

Sample code

Method IF_EX_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER~MAP_CUST_CONT_TO_BP_AND_REL of BADI implementation ZCL_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER in ECC

 

 

 

 

  METHOD if_ex_cvi_custom_mapper~map_cust_cont_to_bp_and_rel.

    DATA: lv_partner_guid TYPE bu_partner_guid,
          lv_partner_no   TYPE bu_partner.

* Convert GUID from CHAR32 to RAW16
    lv_partner_guid    = c_person-header-object_instance-bpartnerguid.

* Fetch contact person ID from SAP CRM (Write your own logic to determine contact person ID)
    CALL FUNCTION 'ZGET_BP_NUMBER_FROM_GUID'
      DESTINATION '<remote destination of SAP CRM>'
      EXPORTING
        iv_guid               = lv_partner_guid
      IMPORTING
        ev_number             = lv_partner_no
      EXCEPTIONS
        system_failure        = 1
        communication_failure = 2
        OTHERS                = 3.

* Change contact person grouping and assign an external number (only when a unique number is determined)
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0 AND lv_partner_no IS NOT INITIAL.
      c_person-central_data-common-data-bp_control-grouping = 'ZCPE'.
      c_person-header-object_instance-bpartner = lv_partner_no.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 

 

Remote-enabled function module ZGET_BP_NUMBER_FROM_GUID in CRM

 

 

 

 

FUNCTION zget_bp_number_from_guid.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(IV_GUID) TYPE  BU_PARTNER_GUID
*"  EXPORTING
*"     VALUE(EV_NUMBER) TYPE  BU_PARTNER
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------


  SELECT SINGLE partner FROM but000 INTO ev_number WHERE partner_guid EQ iv_guid.


ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Building a Sales Organizational Structure in SAP: A Bottom-Up Approach
entry.content:
In any organization, a well-structured sales hierarchy is key to managing operations efficiently. Instead of looking at it from the top down, let’s take a bottom-up approach, starting from the execution teams and working our way up to strategic decision-making.

 

1. Sales Group: The Frontline Sales Force

At the foundation of any sales structure is the Sales Group, which consists of the individuals responsible for selling products in their assigned regions. These teams interact directly with customers, generate sales, and ensure smooth order fulfillment.

Creation: Sales Group

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Definition → Sales and Distribution → Maintain Sales Group

Assignment: Sales Group to Sales Office

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Assignment → Sales and Distribution → Assign Sales Group to Sales Office

2. Sales Office: Managing Regional Operations

Each Sales Group reports to a Sales Office, which acts as the regional hub for sales activities. These offices oversee sales teams, provide support, and manage operational logistics.

For example:

  • The Singapore Sales Office oversees operations in South East Asia.
  • The Germany Sales Office handles sales for East Asia.

Creation: Sales Office

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Definition → Sales and Distribution → Maintain Sales Office

Assignment: Sales Office to Sales Area

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Assignment → Sales and Distribution → Assign Sales Office to Sales Areas

3. Sales Area: Defining Product, Channel, and Organization Alignment

Before products reach customers, they must be assigned to a Sales Area, which is a combination of three critical elements:

  1. Sales Organization (the business entity responsible for sales)
  2. Distribution Channel (the method of selling)
  3. Division (the type of product)

For example:

  • Sales Org: Food + Wholesale Channel + Carcass Division → This area focuses on selling raw food products through wholesale distributors.
  • Sales Org: Food + Retail Channel + Processed Division → This area manages processed food products sold directly to retail customers.

Each Sales Area determines pricing, customer contracts, and order management processes.

Assignment: Sales Area Setup

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Assignment → Sales and Distribution → Set Up Sales Area

4. Division: Organizing Product Categories

A Division categorizes products based on how they are handled in the supply chain. The three divisions in this structure are:

  • Carcass → Raw, unprocessed goods.
  • Processed → Packaged and ready-to-consume products.
  • Live Stock → Direct sale of live animals.

Divisions ensure that sales teams know which products they are responsible for and how they should be distributed.

Creation: Division

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Definition → Logistics – General → Define, Copy, Delete, Check Division (T-code: OVXB)

Assignment: Division to Sales Organization

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Assignment → Sales and Distribution → Assign Division to Sales Organization

5. Distribution Channel: Delivering Products to Customers

Once products are categorized, they need a Distribution Channel to reach customers. The company uses three primary channels:

  • Wholesale → Bulk selling to large-scale buyers.
  • Retail → Direct sales to consumers through stores or e-commerce.
  • Distributor → Third-party partners who handle sales and distribution.

Each channel serves a unique market and requires different sales strategies.

Creation: Distribution Channel

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Definition → Sales and Distribution → Define, Copy, Delete, Check Distribution Channel (T-code: OVXI)

Assignment: Distribution Channel to Sales Organization

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Assignment → Sales and Distribution → Assign Distribution Channel to Sales Organization

6. Sales Organization: The Strategic Backbone

At the highest level, we have the Sales Organization, which oversees all sales functions. In this structure, there are two main Sales Organizations:

  • Food
  • Packaging

These organizations define high-level strategies, pricing policies, and overall business goals for their respective industries.

Creation: Sales Organization

  • Path: Enterprise Structure → Definition → Sales and Distribution → Define, Copy, Delete, Check Sales Organization (T-code: OVX5)

Conclusion

By building the SAP Sales Organizational Structure from the ground up, we can see how each layer supports the one above it. The Sales Groups execute the strategy, the Sales Offices manage regions, the Sales Areas define product-market alignment, and the Sales Organization oversees the entire operation.

This bottom-up approach highlights the importance of each role in ensuring seamless sales execution, efficient distribution, and strong market presence.

entry.title: Advanced SAML support in Cloud Identity Services for corporate IdP (artifact binding)
entry.content:
Hello all,

I'm trying to connect a well-known Dutch Identity Provider (DigiD) from the government to our Cloud Identity Service. However this IdP is using a more secure feature of SAML where it uses back-channel communications. The sensitive data is not sent via the browser of the user but directly from the IdP to the Service provider. This is Artifact Binding as described here in the SAML specifications:

https://docs.oasis-open.org/security/saml/Post2.0/sstc-saml-tech-overview-2.0-cd-02.html#5.1.3.SP-Initiated%20SSO:%20%20POST/Artifact%20Bindings%7Coutline

Does anybody know if this is possible using Cloud Identity Services?

entry.title: Read Audit Report Configuration
entry.content:
Pre-requisites & Configurations

Pre-requisites (RBP - Permissions)

Admin Centre Permissions

  • View Read & Audit Configuration
  • Edit Read & Change Audit Configuration
  • Generate Read Audit Reports

Configurations:

Action 1 🤺  - In Search tool -> Type (Manage Audit Configurations)

Select "Read Audit" tab, followed by enable the "Read Access Logging" toggle button and save it.

 

Quota & Usage: Now you will be start able to see the quota & Usage section.

Key Understanding on Quota & Usage:  

📢 Understanding 1 - Consolidated up to 15 fields only, you can make use of those fields to get audited.

📢 Understanding 2 - Out of 15 fields, the quota for standard fields is only for “5” fields, and the quota for custom fields is only for “10” fields.

📢 Understanding 3 – You cannot make all the existing and enabled fields in the system get read audited; the fields which you see in the “Audit Configuration” only can get audited.

📢 Understanding 4 –

Standard Fields – These are predefined by SAP as Sensitive Personal Data (SPD) fields. 

💡 You can identify with help of predefine label .We don't have control on categorizing it is a Standard / Custom fields.

💡Custom Fields – Apart from pre-identified standard fields, the rest are all custom fields.

Fields from (ONB / Recruitment /HRIS Elements / Standard MDF Objects / Employee Profile)

Action 2 🤺🤺 - Choose  at bottom of the window, will lead ✈️ you to the configuration of different verticals of data fields exist in the system.

The split of field quotas assigned to each section, here 3/3 - standards fields fully utilized, custom field not utilized under this particular section.

Button "Go to Configuration Page" will lead 🚙 you to corresponding configuration page to enable the field as "Sensitive Personal Data"

Action 3 🤺🤺🤺 - How to switch on the field to get read audit.

Go to the respective fields, select "Details", 😎"Log read access"😎 is the button to switch on the field to get audited and save it.

💡- Indicator to know the field is now enabled for audit.

Action 4 🤺🤺🤺🤺 - For standard MDF you need to go to "Configure object definition" to enable the field for read audit.

 

👨‍🎓👩‍🎓- Now run the read audit report through, now you can be able track the info, who are all read the classified "SDP" fields of either their (or) other users. In compliance to quota of 15 fields, you can see the context key & value from "01" to "15". You can only see the field information not with values.

entry.title: We won G2’s 2025 Best Software Awards – thanks to you!
entry.content:
At SAP, we strive to be our best in how we conduct ourselves, build products, and listen to feedback from you, our customers. Today, we are thrilled to announce that SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass, amongst many other amazing SAP products, have been awarded as G2’s 2025 Best Software!

G2, the world’s biggest software marketplace, is visited by 90 million software buyers each year looking at genuine and honest feedback from actual users of software products. The Best Software Awards is an annual award program granted to companies that receive the best marks from their customers, regardless of company size or market category.

SAP Ariba placed on the Best Software for Enterprise Companies list for the first time – beating out all our direct competitors!

SAP Fieldglass placed high on the Best Software and Best Software for Mid-Size Companies lists for the second year in a row. Only 36 companies returned for this award since last year – a 64% churn rate!

In fact, less than 1% (0.87% to be precise) of companies on the G2 platform place within these awards. Truly, only the best!

But the fun doesn’t end there. SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass are consistently ranked as Leaders within our respective categories during the quarterly G2 Grid rankings. Check out the latest G2 reports for the G2 Winter 2025 Procure-to-Pay Grid and the G2 Winter 2025 Vendor Management Grid!

 

 

Not only are these awards a testament to the dedication of our team, but more importantly, it’s a reflection of your trust. Every review placed on the G2 platform fuels our commitment to innovation, improvement, and giving you the best possible experience using SAP products. Thank you to our amazing customers and community!
